name: Lac d'Aude par le Mont Llaret – trail loop starting from Pla del Mir (Les Angles) – Parc naturel régional des Pyrénées Catalanes
name_original: Lac d'Aude par le Mont Llaret – boucle trail au départ du Pla del Mir (Les Angles) – Parc naturel régional des Pyrénées Catalanes
region_intro: Hiking around Les Angles offers diverse terrain within the Catalan Pyrenees Regional Nature Park, characterized by high mountain peaks, serene lakes, and expansive forests. The region features varied landscapes, from pine forests to exposed mountain ridges, providing a range of environments for different hiking experiences. Notable natural features include Pic Carlit, standing at 2,921 meters, and the significant Lac des Bouillouses at 2,016 meters. Best hiking trails around Les Angles The most popular hiking route is Pic Carlit – hike starting from the parking lot of lac des Bouillouses – Parc naturel régional des Pyrénées Catalanes, an 8.9 miles (14.3 km) difficult trail that takes 6 hours 31 minutes to complete. This route ascends to the highest peak in the Pyrénées-Orientales, offering panoramic views. Another top favourite among local hikers is Mont Llaret and Lac d'Aude Loop, a difficult 8.9 miles (14.3 km) path. This trail features significant elevation gain and explores the area around Lac d'Aude. Local hikers also love the Estany Negre (Black Lake) – Lake Vallell loop from Lac des Bouillouses, a 7.3 miles (11.8 km) trail leading through high-altitude lakes and mountain scenery, often completed in about 4 hours 8 minutes. Hiking around Les Angles is defined by majestic peaks, numerous high-altitude lakes, and diverse trails through pine forests and mountain ridges. The network offers options for all ability levels, from easy paths to challenging alpine ascents. The routes in Les Angles are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 1,000 reviews. More than 12,000 hikers have used komoot to explore Les Angles's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Les Angles?
A: There are over 120 hiking routes available around Les Angles, catering to various skill levels. This includes 35 easy trails, 61 moderate routes, and 28 challenging hikes for experienced trekkers.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes in Les Angles?
A: The terrain around Les Angles is diverse, ranging from serene pine forests to exposed high mountain ridges. You'll encounter varied landscapes within the Catalan Pyrenees Regional Nature Park, featuring significant elevation changes, especially on routes leading to peaks like Pic Carlit.

Q: Are there any easy or family-friendly hiking options in Les Angles?
A: Yes, Les Angles offers 35 easy trails suitable for families and beginners. These paths provide a gentle introduction to the region's natural beauty without requiring extensive hiking experience. For specific recommendations, you can explore the easy routes available on komoot.

Q: What are some notable natural features or landmarks to see while hiking?
A: Hikers can explore stunning natural features such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4537562" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lac des Bouillouses&lt;/a&gt;, a significant high-altitude lake, and other picturesque ponds like Estany Negre. The region is also home to &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2639154" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Col de la Llose&lt;/a&gt;, offering scenic vistas, and the majestic Pic Carlit, the highest peak in the Pyrénées-Orientales.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Les Angles?
A: Yes, there are several circular routes. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10538524" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Estany Negre (Black Lake) – Lake Vallell loop from Lac des Bouillouses&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 7.3-mile (11.8 km) trail. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/36035216" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lake Aude – Lake Bouillousses loop from Lac des Bouillouses&lt;/a&gt;, also a moderate 7.1-mile (11.5 km) route.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Les Angles?
A: The hiking routes in Les Angles are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 1,000 reviews. Hikers often praise the majestic peaks, numerous high-altitude lakes, and diverse trails through pine forests and mountain ridges, offering options for all ability levels.

Q: Is it possible to hike to Pic Carlit from Les Angles?
A: Yes, the ascent to Pic Carlit, the highest peak in the Pyrénées-Orientales, is a challenging but rewarding hike. A popular starting point is from the parking lot of Lac des Bouillouses, as seen in the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e958412567/pic-carlit-hike-starting-from-the-parking-lot-of-lac-des-bouillouses-parc-naturel-regional-des-pyrenees-catalanes" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pic Carlit – hike starting from the parking lot of lac des Bouillouses&lt;/a&gt; route, offering vast panoramic views.

Q: Are there any trails that explore the area around Lac d'Aude?
A: Absolutely. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e958743036/mont-llaret-and-lac-d-aude-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mont Llaret and Lac d'Aude Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 8.9-mile (14.3 km) path that features significant elevation gain and explores the beautiful area around Lac d'Aude, offering stunning mountain scenery.

Q: What is the best time of year for hiking in Les Angles?
A: Les Angles is a four-season resort. While winter offers snowshoeing, the best time for hiking without snow is typically from late spring to early autumn. During these months, the high-altitude lakes are accessible, and the weather is generally favorable for exploring the diverse mountain terrain.

Q: Are there any specific permits required for hiking in the Catalan Pyrenees Regional Nature Park?
A: Generally, permits are not required for day hikes on marked trails within the Catalan Pyrenees Regional Nature Park. However, for specific protected areas or overnight stays, it's always advisable to check with the local park authorities for any specific regulations or requirements.

Q: Can I find trails that pass by multiple high-altitude ponds?
A: Yes, the region is known for its numerous high-altitude lakes and ponds. The area around Lac des Bouillouses is particularly rich in these, with trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e958413312/esquits-ponds-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Esquits Ponds Loop&lt;/a&gt; offering a chance to explore several picturesque bodies of water in one hike.
tips: Mount Llaret, which dominates Lake Aude (source of the river) at an altitude of 2,376 m, is quite accessible from the resort of Les Angles and its hiking trails.

Lake Aude or Estany d'Auda is a natural lake in the French Pyrenees. It is located at the foot of the rock of Aude, in the town of Angles, in the department of Pyrénées-Orientales. It gives birth to the Aude river.
